channel.title: DutchNews.nl
channel.description: DutchNews.nl brings daily news from The Netherlands in English

item.title: Two arrested after body of man found in house in Arnhem
item.description:
Two arrested after body of man found in house in Arnhem

September 22, 2025

Two people have been arrested after the body of a man was found in a house in Arnhem.

Police said a 23-year-old man from Zutphen and a 31-year-old woman of no fixed abode were being questioned following the discovery of the body in Sterappelgaard, a street in the Zuid district, on Sunday.

Police described the circumstances as suspicious, but gave no further details about the man, such as his age or relationship with the two suspects.

The area around the street has been cordoned off with tape and a mobile police office has been placed outside for the on-site team.
item.dc:creator: Gordon Darroch

item.title: House prices rose more than 8% in August, continuing trend
item.description:
House prices rose more than 8% in August, continuing trend

September 22, 2025

The average house was 8.6% more expensive in August then a year earlier, according to latest figures from Dutch statistics office CBS.

For an owner-occupied dwelling, the average price in August was 486,190 euros. When compared to July 2025, however, the average price decreased 0.5%.

Earlier this month, data released by the CBS showed that house prices have continued to rise overall in the Netherlands and the upward trend is visible in every local authority area in the country.

Top of the list is Albrandswaard near Rotterdam, where house prices are now almost 22% higher than a year ago, the CBS said in a second-quarter update.

But even in places long at the bottom of the list, such as Hattem in Gelderland and Reusel-De Mierden in Noord-Brabant, prices have increased by a fraction.

House prices have been rising since 2014, reaching an initial peak in July 2022. Prices dropped for nearly a year before beginning to rise again in June 2023. Prices are now 13.5% higher than the summer 2022 record.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell

item.title: Campaign starts to boost voting among Dutch Moroccans
item.description:
Campaign starts to boost voting among Dutch Moroccans

September 22, 2025

More people with a Moroccan background should exercise their right to vote, Moroccan Dutch organisation (SMN) said at the launch of a campaign to encourage participation in the upcoming national elections on October 29.

“The turnout among our community has been very low for years,” SMN coordinator Habib el Kaddouri told broadcaster NOS.

“The political climate is prejudicing many Dutch Moroccans. There is little trust. In the last few years, comments have been made and legislation introduced that have put people on the defensive,” he said.

There are no exact turnout figures for the different population groups because of privacy rules, according to political science lecturer Lucas Vermeulen.

“Research has shown that the turnout among people with Surinamese and Moroccan backgrounds is lower than average,” he said. One of the reasons is a feeling that politicians do not represent their interests, he added.

Some 20% of voters have a migration background, Vermeulen said. “The Turkish community is known to be well-organised, with big networks that motivate people to go and vote. Other communities lack those networks. Dutch Turks vote more often than Dutch Moroccans.”

The SMN hopes to stop the trend, El Kaddouri said. “If you don’t use your vote, you are giving space to parties that don’t care about your rights and interests,” he said.

The campaign, hashtagged #pakjestemterug (reclaim your vote) comprises videos and podcasts which can be seen on social media from October 1. There will also be meetings across the country.

Vermeulen is not confident the campaign will lead to a higher turnout among Dutch Moroccans. “But to emphasise the importance of participation is always meaningful,” he said.

“Many people feel they don’t belong. They see politicians who are anti-Islam or who exclude people and they keep away. But democracy only works if everyone participates.”

“We hope we can inspire people to vote,” El Kaddouri said. “Vote for whatever party you like, but join in the democratic process.”
item.dc:creator: Hanneke Sanou

item.title: Nearly half of Dutch voters say Gaza is an issue in the election
item.description:
Nearly half of Dutch voters say Gaza is an issue in the election

September 22, 2025

Two-fifths of voters in the Netherlands say the conflict in Gaza will be a factor in deciding how they cast their vote in next month’s election.

In a poll by Motivaction commissioned by human rights organisations, 42% said Gaza would influence their choice of party either slightly or a great deal, while 39% said it would make little or no difference.

For the first time more than half of Dutch voters say Israel is committing genocide in Gaza, with the proportion rising from 48% to 54% since April.

One in eight voters (12%) disagreed with the statement, while one in five did not know or had no opinion – down from 29% at the last poll.

Roelien Sasse, executive director of PAX, one of the organisations that commissioned the research, said: “The Dutch population are seeing war crime after war crime in Gaza and want the government to draw a clear red line.”

In July two Israeli NGOs, B’Tselem and Physicians for Human Rights, described the situation in Gaza as genocide, adding their voices to international organisations such as Amnesty International.

The International Association of Genocide Scholars and a United Nations investigative committee have both recently endorsed the term, but Dutch foreign affairs minister David van Weel has said the government will only so once there is a judgment by the International Court of Justice, which will take years.

A majority of Dutch voters say the government should impose sanctions against Israel, with 57% calling for a ban on trade with illegal settlements on the West Bank. Some 54% say the EU should no longer give Israel any advantages on trade, while 53% support an embargo on arms exports.

The cabinet recently revoked three licences for arms exports to Israel and said new permits are “more or less excluded”, but it has stopped short of enforcing an absolute ban.
item.dc:creator: Gordon Darroch

item.title: Dutch police shoot dead armed teenager, 15, after fatbike theft
item.description:
Dutch police shoot dead armed teenager, 15, after fatbike theft

September 21, 2025

Dutch police have shot dead a 15-year-old boy following the theft of a fatbike in the Zuid-Holland town of Capelle aan den IJssel, east of Rotterdam.

The shooting took place on Sunday afternoon at a branch of fast food snack bar McDonald’s and was witnessed by dozens of people, including many young children.

The police arrived at the scene of the reported theft at around 4:15 on Sunday afternoon, and eyewitnesses had told the police that the victim had threatened to use a gun.



He had gone by the time the police arrived but was later spotted by police in the neighbourhood of the McDonald’s branch, where he was warned to stop. Instead, he ran off, at which point the police shot at him.

Camera footage shows how a police officer fired at least four shots at a distance of just a few metres from the youngster on the fast food restaurant terrace, RTL Nieuws reported.

The national police force has now started an investigation into the shooting, which is standard procedure when people are injured or killed by the police.

RTL said the youngster did have a gun, which is now being looked at by forensic experts. The police have not yet issued a formal statement.



A police spokesman told broadcaster NOS one other youngster who was involved in the theft of the bike was arrested at the scene of the shooting. A second teenager involved in the robbery is said to have turned himself into the police.

A McDonald’s worker who was having a break next to the emergency exit told RTL that she saw everything happen. “Suddenly lads appeared on a fatbike,” she said. “They put it next to the door and then one of them pulled out a pistol. He was half a metre away and I thought ‘shit, I need to go in’.”

Capelle aan den IJssel mayor Joost Manasuma called for calm. He said many young children had witnessed the shooting and “that makes the impact even worse”.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pascoe

item.title: PSV-Ajax ends all square, late AZ penalty denies Feyenoord win
item.description:
PSV-Ajax ends all square, late AZ penalty denies Feyenoord win

September 21, 2025

PSV and Ajax shared the spoils in the first heavyweight clash of the Eredivisie season in Eindhoven, as a late goal by Oscar Gloukh denied the home side the win their enterprising play deserved.

PSV dominated the opening half-hour with more than 70% possession, but their only reward was Ismail Saibari’s headed goal in the ninth minute.

equalised when referee Serdar Gözübüyük awarded a penalty after studying  video review of Sergiño Dest’s tackle on Oliver Edvardsen, with Kenneth Taylor converting the spot-kick.

PSV suffered a blow when Ruben van Bommel was taken off shortly afterwards, but still finished the first half stronger and should have taken the lead straight after the restart when Saibari screwed a volley wide from close range.

Yarek Gasiorowski looked to have snatched the win when he headed PSV’s second goal nine minutes from time and then blocked a shot from Mika Godts. But Gloukh had the last word with a shot between the legs of Matej Kovár in the 88th minute.

Feyenoord drop points

Leaders Feyenoord dropped their first points of the season at AZ, who equalised with a 98th-minute penalty by Mexx Meerdinck after the visitors had come back from 2-1 down in the second half.

The teams went into the half-time interval after Sem Steijn scored a penalty for Feyenoord to cancel out Ro-Zangelo Daal’s magnificent curling shot in the 19th minute.

Denso Kasius restored the lead in the 51st minute when he drifted into the area and stroked the ball into the bottom corner. Feyenoord quickly replied through Jordan Bos’s deflected strike and looked to be heading for a sixth consecutive league victory when Anis Hadji Moussa rounded off a slick passing move in the 78th minute.

But a rash foul in injury time by Jaden Slory gave Meerdink the chance to salvage a point.

Groningen fourth

Groningen moved up to fourth place with a win over newcomers Telstar, as Younes Taha’s free-kick and a late strike by Oscar Zawada proved the difference between the teams.

New Twente coach John van den Brom watched his team romp to a 5-1 win at Sparta, with four goals in the second half to double their haul for the season so far.

Heracles remain pointless at the bottom of the table as Sydney van Hooijdonk’s header in the 85th minute completed a comeback for NAC Breda. Heracles took an early lead when Yvandro Borges Sanches ran from deep in his own half to score, before Boy Kemper equalised from a corner in the second half.

Ihattaren on target

Mohamed Ihattaren’s 15th-minute goal was enough for Fortuna Sittard to claim the points against Utrecht and move above their opponents into sixth place.

Milan Smit scored twice in the last 10 minutes to earn Go Ahead Eagles the win in the IJssel derby against PEC Zwolle, who were reduced to 10 men after just 90 seconds when Jamiro Monteiro was shown a red card for a dangerous high kick.

Another late goal decided the battle of the promoted clubs as Lewis Schouten’s header gave Excelsior a 2-1 win at Volendam, while Heerenveen survived a second-half fightback by NEC to record their first win of the season.

Results

Friday

Sparta Rotterdam 1-5 Twente Enschede

Saturday

Fortuna Sittard 1-0 Utrecht

Groningen 2-0 Telstar

NAC Breda 2-1 Heracles Almelo

Volendam 1-2 Excelsior

Sunday

AZ Alkmaar 3-3 Feyenoord

Heerenveen 3-2 NEC Nijmegen

PEC Zwolle 0-2 Go Ahead Eagles

PSV Eindhoven 2-2 Ajax
item.dc:creator: Gordon Darroch

item.title: Dutch men are surprise bronze medallists in 4×100 metres relay
item.description:
Dutch men are surprise bronze medallists in 4×100 metres relay

September 21, 2025

The Dutch men’s 4×100 metres relay team were the surprise winners of the bronze medal on the last day of the World Athletics Championships in Tokyo on Sunday.

Taymir Burnet, Elvis Afrifa, Xavi Mo-Ajok and Nsikak Ekpo set a new Dutch record of 37.81 in the race, which was won by the US in 37.29. Canada took the silver.

“We qualified as 16th, and we leave in third place, we set a new Dutch record in the rain. We deserve this,” said Mo-Ajok after the race.



The Dutch women’s 4×400 metres relay team, who won the title two years ago and silver at the Olympics, also won the bronze medal, with Femke Bol picking up her third podium place of the games. America won gold and Jamaica took the silver.

The Netherlands ended the competition in fourth place in the medals table with two gold, two silver and two bronze.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pascoe

item.title: Anti-immigration riot arrest total hits 37, four police hurt
item.description:
Anti-immigration riot arrest total hits 37, four police hurt

September 21, 2025

Police said on Sunday that 37 people were arrested during Saturday’s rioting at an anti-immigration demonstration in the centre of The Hague, and 27 of them remain in custody.

Four police officers, rather than two, were also injured in the fighting, according to a police statement. “More than 1,000 rioters split off from the main demonstration against asylum policy,” the statement said. “They did not shy away from using violence against the police, journalists and their surroundings.”

The police have now launched an investigation to try to identify other rioters and have appealed for photos and camera footage to help with their inquiries.



Police used water cannon and tear gas to break up the group, who set fire to a police car, attacked the offices of political party D66, and attempted to storm the parliamentary complex which is currently closed for renovations.

The Hague mayor Jan van Zanen had earlier said the rioters were “hooligans” who had come from “all over the country and deliberately sought confrontation with the police.”

But D66 leader Rob Jetten told a television current affairs show on Sunday it was wrong to say football hooligans were behind the trouble. “They were not shouting ‘go ADO’ or ‘go FC Utrecht,’ but they were chanting about taking back the Netherlands,” he said.



Some of the demonstrators were also carrying the “prinsen” flag, which has become a symbol of the far right and was used by the Dutch Nazi party during WWII. Others were heard to shout antisemitic slogans and do Nazi salutes, broadcaster NOS said.

One waitress at a cafe which was overrun by rioters told NOS the gang were shouting “get rid of the foreigners” and “Sieg Heil”. One waitress trying to protect a guest was tazered by a rioter, she said. “It was traumatic.”

The demonstration was organised by a young woman who calls herself Els Rechts, or right-wing Els and is a big fan of far-right leader Geert Wilders. Els told reporters she is “shocked” that her “peaceful protest” had been taken over and that she would never have organised it if she had known what would happen.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pascoe

item.title: 30 arrested in The Hague’s anti-immigration riots, more expected
item.description:
30 arrested in The Hague’s anti-immigration riots, more expected

September 21, 2025

At least 30 people were arrested during Saturday’s rioting during an anti-immigration demonstration in the centre of The Hague, and more arrests will follow, police said at a press conference.

Two police officers were also injured in the fighting, which was instigated by “hooligans,” The Hague’s mayor Jan van Zanen said. The violence, Van Zanen told reporters, “was unprecedented and unworthy of the Netherlands.”

“Groups of hooligans from all over the country deliberately sought confrontation with the police,” he said. “And they used extreme violence.”



The police have now set up a special team to track down the troublemakers, focusing on those who committed violence against the police and journalists, as well as vandalizing property.

Local police chief Karin Krukkert said that there were signals people “with the wrong intentions” would attend the rally on the Malieveld near The Hague’s main railway station, “but we had not predicted such a big group using such violence.”

Police used water cannon and tear gas to break up the group of rioters, who set fire to a police car, attacked the offices of political party D66, attempted to storm the parliamentary complex, and injured several journalists.

The trouble began when a group of 1,500 protesters attempted to blockade the nearby A12 motorway, ignoring appeals from organisers not to do so. Many of them were dressed in black, some covering their faces with balaclavas.

Some of those who attended displayed far-right symbols such as the prinsjesvlag, a forerunner of the Dutch national flag with an orange stripe, which is associated with the pre-war Dutch Nazi party (NSB).

Demonstrators clashed with police on the Malieveld, throwing stones and bottles and setting a police car on fire. A press photographer covering the protest was taken to hospital after being punched in the face.

One group broke away at around 3pm and headed towards the Binnenhof parliamentary complex, which is currently being renovated, where they were involved in a stand-off with riot police.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pascoe

item.title: Dutch comedian goes viral with Disney satire over Kimmel sacking
item.description:
Dutch comedian goes viral with Disney satire over Kimmel sacking

September 20, 2025

A short video satirising Disney’s decision to pull a popular talk show because of the host’s comments on the murder of Maga campaigner Charlie Kirk has gone viral for Dutch TV presenter Arjen Lubach.

The film, which shows snippets of classic Disney films with new texts based on president Donald Trump statements and opinions, has been viewed over 10 million times since it was first broadcast on Thursday, the Lubach editorial team has told news agency ANP.

ABC, which is part of the Disney empire, took the Jimmy Kimmel talk show off air this week.



The film includes Belle’s father in Beauty and the Beast saying he would date his daughter, and Princess Anna from Frozen stating that global warming is not true because her snowman friend Olaf has not melted. In another section, Aladdin is taken away by ICE immigration officials.


In his commentary, Lubach says that even a giant like Disney is terrified of Trump. “Everything has to be done exactly as Trump likes it,” he said.

Disney has not yet commented on the pastiche.

In 2017 Lubach had another big hit in the US when he urged Americans to put “the Netherlands second”.

That satirical video clip introduced the Netherlands to Trump ‘in a way he would understand’
item.dc:creator: Robin Pascoe
